intro: 395 AI items this week (65 India-relevant) across 10 sources.

items.summary: arXiv:2607.00233v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: How do two agents invent a shared language from scratch? In a Lewis signaling game, a sender and receiver must coordinate on a code using only their interaction history. We study five memory architectures across varying channel c…

items.title: AI Native Games: A Survey and Roadmap
items.summary: arXiv:2607.00527v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generative AI now enables games to produce dialogue, quests, characters, images, and worlds at runtime. Yet generation alone does not make a game AI-native, nor does it guarantee playability. This paper defines AI-native games by…

items.title: He bought a ₹3.5 lakh bank server. Now he makes ₹27 lakh a month with AI
items.summary: Raj is 27, writes code in a one bedroom flat in Bangalore, and a few months ago he did something almost nobody thinks to do.

items.title: Future-ready teaching: How AI is transforming education in India
items.summary: At a Microsoft Elevate webinar, educators, non-profit leaders, and technologists came together to explore and discuss how artificial intelligence can make learning more personal, inclusive, and human.

items.title: At Atlassian Unleash, AI speed reinforced tough parameters of context, trust and resilience
items.summary: At Atlassian Unleash, leaders examined how organisations must rebuild workflows, accountability and engineering foundations as software creation accelerates from open agentic infrastructure to context-aware enterprise AI.

items.title: Is SpaceX building an AI phone? Elon Musk calls report ‘utterly false’
Michael Cronin wants Couchbase APAC to win the AI data race
items.summary: Michael Cronin, Managing Director for Asia Pacific at Couchbase, is driving regional growth by building high-performing teams and expanding the company's presence.

items.title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30M of his own money to build AI alternative to Microsoft Office
items.summary: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office, Google Apps with AI.

items.title: SK Hynix to spend $64 billion on chip plants under broader AI-driven investment plan
items.summary: The projects in the country's central city of Cheongju outlined on Thursday come ​under a $2.1 trillion plan unveiled by the chipmaker and its local rival Samsung Electronics on Monday that also included a new chip cluster in the southwest and existing projects.

items.title: OpenAI offers 5% stake to Trump administration: report
items.summary: ​​The proposed ​arrangement would involve other U.S. ​AI companies handing over a similar ​stake to ⁠the government, ‌The Financial Times reported, ​adding that ⁠it was not clear if ​the other AI ​firms would be willing to do so.

careers.note: Demand snapshot for 2026. ML Engineer is the most in-demand AI title; agentic-AI and deep-learning skills carry the highest pay premiums; Python + one cloud is the baseline. Specialisation beats generalisation for freshers.

paths.note: #1 AI title — build, deploy & maintain models in production. Best all-round entry into AI.
RAG, tool-use & multi-agent systems. ~40% of enterprise apps will embed AI agents by year-end.
Neural nets for vision & speech. Highest premiums; usually needs strong maths / grad study.
Ship & scale models on the cloud. Cloud AI certs add 20–25% to salary.
Foundational, widest entry door; a reliable first step into AI.
Imaging, robotics, autonomous systems, medical AI.
Powers chatbots, search & agents — the hottest application area.
5–8 yrs experience; $140k–$332k bands. Translates AI into business value.
Alignment, evals, red-teaming & policy — newer but expanding fast.

fresher.why: The lingua franca of ML — non-negotiable.
Linear algebra, probability & calculus — enough to reason about models.
Regression, classification, evaluation, the ML workflow.
Build & train neural nets; PyTorch dominates research & jobs.
Ship a small LLM app — the most visible skill to recruiters now.
Real AI work is mostly data; SQL is everywhere.
AWS leads (~40% of postings), then Azure & GCP. Version everything.
A deployed project that solves a real problem is the #1 differentiator.

experienced.why: Docker, CI/CD, monitoring, model/versioning — production is where value lives.
Multi-agent orchestration, tool-use, evals — the surging frontier.
AWS/Azure/GCP architecture; certs add a 20–25% salary premium.
Adapt, measure and harden models for a domain.
Cost & latency engineering for large models.
Trade-offs across data, latency, cost & reliability at scale.
CV, NLP, recsys, healthcare — depth beats breadth at senior levels.
Leading teams and tying models to measurable outcomes.

profile.name: Harman Singh Sandhu
profile.blurb: I build AI/ML projects and write about them. This site is one of them — an automated daily AI digest.

repos.name: ai-weekly-report
repos.desc: My personalized AI newsletter that runs on an agentic AI

repos.name: Ace-rentals
repos.desc: Ace Rentals: A full-stack property rental platform built with Node.js, Express, and MongoDB. Features user authentication, image uploads, geocoding, reviews, and secure account management. Inspired by Airbnb.
